Complement to PMFBY for localised risks
Rs 7,000-10,000/ha compensation

title: Godhan Yojana
summary: Purchases cow dung at Rs 2/kg for organic fertiliser production, promoting cattle care and rural sanitation.
details: Cow dung purchased at Rs 2/kg at Godhan Kendras (gram panchayat level). Processed into vermicompost sold at subsidised rates. Women SHGs operate Kendras. Free vet check-ups, deworming.
key_points: Cow dung purchase at Rs 2/kg for vermicompost
Godhan Kendras operated by women SHGs
Free vet check-ups during collection

title: Birsa Krishi Yojana
summary: Comprehensive farmer support with free HYV seeds, fertilisers, pesticides, and soil health services.
details: Free HYV seeds for kharif/rabi, free fertiliser kits per SHC recommendations, free bio-pesticides. Mobile advisories. Free soil testing with doorstep collection. Custom hiring centres at 50% subsidy.
key_points: Free HYV seeds, fertilisers, bio-pesticides for small farmers
Soil testing with doorstep collection
Custom hiring centres at 50% subsidy

title: Mukhyamantri Prayag Yojana
summary: Demand-driven skill training in 200+ trades for unemployed youth with placement guarantee.
details: 200+ trades: IT, healthcare, retail, hospitality, construction, automotive, solar, drone piloting. 1-6 months with Rs 1,500/month stipend. NSQF certification. 70% placement guarantee. 1 lakh youth/year target.
key_points: 200+ job-oriented courses based on industry demand
Rs 1,500/month stipend + NSQF certification
70% placement guarantee — 1 lakh youth/year

title: Jal Jeevan Hariyali Yojana
summary: Integrated water conservation, afforestation, and climate resilience scheme.
details: 2 lakh water harvesting structures + 50 crore trees target. Check dams, farm ponds, stop dams, rooftop harvesting. Watershed development. Plantation of fruit/timber trees. MGNREGA convergence.
key_points: Integrated water conservation + afforestation + livelihood
2 lakh water structures + 50 crore trees
MGNREGA convergence — Gram Sabha action plans

title: Mukhyamantri Sinchan Yojana
summary: Subsidised pumpsets, micro-irrigation, and borewells for small and marginal farmers.
details: 90% subsidy on solar pumps, 75% on electric/diesel pumps. 90% on drip/sprinkler. 100% borewell subsidy in water-scarce blocks (up to Rs 50,000). Priority to SC/ST farmers. 2 lakh additional ha/year irrigation.
key_points: 90% subsidy solar pumps, 75% electric pumps
90% on drip/sprinkler micro-irrigation
100% borewell in water-scarce blocks

title: Aspirational Blocks Programme
summary: Accelerated development in most backward blocks with health, education, agriculture, and infrastructure convergence.
details: Identifies most backward blocks based on composite indicators. Nodal IAS officer per block. Priority funding from all CSS and state schemes. Monthly dashboard monitoring. Initially 50 blocks.
key_points: Most backward blocks identified for accelerated development
Nodal IAS officer per block
Monthly dashboard monitoring

title: Mukhyamantri Rajya Patra Yojana
summary: State social pension for destitute elderly, widows, disabled, and bereaved families not covered under NSAP.
details: Rs 600/month (Rs 1,000 for 80+). Covers elderly 60+, widows 40+, disabled 40%+, deserted women. Funeral assistance Rs 5,000. Family benefit Rs 20,000 on breadwinner death. Gram Sabha identification + Aadhaar DBT.
key_points: Rs 600/month (Rs 1,000 for 80+) for non-NSAP beneficiaries
Covers elderly, widows, disabled, deserted women
Gram Sabha identification + Aadhaar DBT

title: Krushak Assistance for Livelihood and Income Augmentation
summary: Flagship Odisha farmer scheme providing Rs 10,000/year to small/marginal farmers including landless labourers.
details: Rs 10,000/year in two instalments (kharif/rabi). Covers landless agri labourers — more inclusive than PM-KISAN. Life insurance Rs 2 lakh. Additional assistance for elderly/disabled farmers. 70+ lakh families benefit.

More inclusive than PM-KISAN
Life insurance cover of Rs 2 lakh

title: Mamata Yojana
summary: Odisha cash transfer scheme for pregnant/lactating women providing Rs 5,000 for first two live births.
details: Rs 5,000 in four instalments: Rs 1,500 (early registration), Rs 1,500 (3 ANC), Rs 1,000 (institutional delivery), Rs 1,000 (immunisation). Pradates central PMMVY. State funded.
key_points: Rs 5,000 in four instalments for first two births
Conditional on registration, ANC, delivery, immunisation
Predates PMMVY — state funded

title: Biju Kalal Yojana
aka: Biju Kalal, Health Insurance for BPL
summary: Health insurance for BPL families in Odisha providing cashless treatment up to Rs 5 lakh for serious illnesses.
details: Rs 5 lakh for serious illnesses (cancer, heart, kidney), Rs 50,000 for general hospitalisation, Rs 5,000 OPD. No age bar, no family cap, pre-existing diseases covered. Free medicines at all govt hospitals.
key_points: Rs 5 lakh for serious illnesses + Rs 50,000 general
No age bar or family cap
70+ lakh BPL families covered

title: Biju Pucca Ghar Yojana
summary: Housing assistance to rural/urban BPL families not covered under central housing schemes.
details: Rs 1.20-1.30 lakh per house. Additional Rs 12,000 for toilet. Women co-ownership mandatory. Two rooms, kitchen, verandah design. Linked with DAY-NRLM SHGs for livelihood. 15+ lakh houses built.
key_points: Rs 1.20-1.30 lakh for uncovered BPL families
15+ lakh houses constructed
Women co-ownership mandatory

title: Biju Slisha Yojana
summary: Comprehensive education scholarship for SC/ST/OBC students from Class I to professional courses.
details: Maintenance allowance Rs 100-1,500/month. Tuition fee reimbursement up to Rs 25,000/year for professional courses. Textbook grant Rs 500-1,000/year for I-X. Income ceiling Rs 2.5 lakh (SC/ST), Rs 1 lakh (OBC).

Maintenance Rs 100-1,500/month + tuition reimbursement
Online e-kalyan portal

title: Biju Swasthya Kalyan Yojana
summary: Universal health assurance for all Odisha residents with enhanced cover up to Rs 10 lakh for women-headed families.
details: Rs 5 lakh general cover, Rs 10 lakh for women-headed families. Rs 500/year OPD per person. All hospitalisation. 1+ crore families. Free drugs and diagnostics at all government hospitals for all citizens.

Rs 10 lakh for women-headed families
1+ crore families covered

Focus on coastal and tribal areas
Fisheries, cashew, lac cultivation support

Revolving fund and 0% interest loans
Leadership training and digital literacy

Monthly living allowance

Awareness on women safety laws
Emergency helpline integration
